title: Read-aloud audio narration of core lessons (low-literacy access)
context: Audio narration of the core lessons extends access to learners with low literacy, visual impairments, or learning differences who benefit from audio-first content. Text-aligned audio (synchronized with the text) enables follow-along reading. The narration must be reviewed for plain-language quality and carry the same not-advice framing as the text. It is a CC-BY-4.0 licensed dataset derived from the curriculum text. Voice talent or text-to-speech must be selected without endorsing any specific tool or service; the implementation approach is left to the task contributor.
objective: Produce and publish text-aligned read-aloud audio narration for all core curriculum lessons (Budgeting, Saving, Debt, Banking, Scams modules + Glossary) as an openly-licensed accessibility dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio narration produced for all five core modules and Glossary v1; text-aligned (timestamps or segment markers match lesson text sections)
Narration is clear, plain, and reviewed for plain-language quality at or equivalent to the text (no added jargon or advice phrasing)
Audio files are openly licensed CC-BY-4.0; voice/narrator credited (with consent) in the dataset metadata
Dataset is structured (e.g., JSON manifest mapping lesson segment to audio file + timestamp) for reuse by educators and other OER projects
Not-advice framing phrases are present in the audio at the same points as the text; linter-equivalent check on the narration script confirms no advice phrasing added
Audio quality is sufficient for low-bandwidth streaming and offline download (compressed format documented)
output: An openly-licensed read-aloud audio narration dataset (data/audio/) with text-aligned manifests covering the core curriculum modules, ready for integration into the static site and for download as a standalone OER asset.
